Details:
Data from one study are suggestive that the ability to remove glyphosate using membrane filtration is dependent upon the membrane’s molecular weight cut-off (MWCO). The study found that 50% and > 99% removal of glyphosate from contaminated river water could be achieved with 1000 and 500 dalton MWCO membranes, respectively.
Furthermore, no removal was achieved from river water using 10,000 dalton MWCO ultrafiltration membranes or 0.45 μm microfiltration filters.
